queue_signal.headline_snippet: Railway Ministry orders high-level inquiry into Trivandrum-New Delhi Rajdhani Express fire incident - Newsonair

record_snapshot.incident_name: Trivandrum-New Delhi Rajdhani Express fire incident

duplicate_guard.reason: No exact or near-duplicate case record was found in content/cases.
evidence: Repo search for Rajdhani, Trivandrum, Thiruvananthapuram, and train fire terms returned no matching case file.
The nearest existing train-fire case in the repo is morena-train-fire-rumour-fatalities, which concerns a different incident and location.

identity_gate.notes: The frozen Akashvani News article matches the queue signal on incident type, date, and railway route. It describes a fire in two rear coaches of the Trivandrum-New Delhi Rajdhani Express near Nagda of the Kota Division, with no passengers injured.
supporting_quotes: The Railway Ministry has ordered a high-level inquiry into the fire incident involving two rear coaches of the Trivandrum-New Delhi Rajdhani Express.
At around 5:15 this morning, there was a fire in two rear coaches, which included one guard coach and one AC 3 Tier in the Trivandrum-New Delhi Rajdhani near Nagda of the Kota Division.
no passengers were injured in the incident

field_updates.title: Trivandrum-New Delhi Rajdhani Express Fire Incident
field_updates.accused_gap: No public accused named or arrests reported in the frozen source set.
field_updates.summary: A fire broke out in the two rear coaches of the Trivandrum-New Delhi Rajdhani Express near Nagda on 17 May 2026. The Railway Ministry ordered a high-level inquiry, the train stopped automatically after the safety system worked, the crew detached the affected coaches, no passengers were injured, and an audit of coach fire systems and drivers was launched across Indian Railways.

timeline.event: News On AIR reported a fire in two rear coaches of the Trivandrum-New Delhi Rajdhani Express near Nagda of the Kota Division.

The Ministry said the coach safety system worked immediately, the train automatically stopped, the two affected rear coaches were detached, no passengers were injured, and a railways-wide audit of coach fire systems and drivers was launched.

draft_diff: Created a new no-action case from the Akashvani News fire report, keeping deaths null because the article does not state a death count and recording injured as 0 because it explicitly says no passengers were injured.

critic_review.notes: The case file is conservative where needed: deaths remain null, injured is supported as 0, and no unsupported accused or compensation claims were introduced.

validation.notes: The case file frontmatter parsed cleanly, the frozen source artifact was written directly from the source article, duplicate search found no near-match in content/cases, and the Hugo build completed successfully.
